frame_label: Dress Code: Traditional = Respectable, Other = Questionable
generated_comment: Arre Neha, this saree look suits you much more yaar. Indian clothes mein ek alag hi grace aur decency hoti hai, these western outfits don't give the same impression.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it links a woman's clothing to her 'grace' and 'decency,' implying traditional dress makes her more respectable than western clothing. It encodes the backhanded compliment and traditional-wear-is-superior surface manifestation.
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 35. Married. A man in Mumbai. Your wife Neha is fair skinned. You are at a party and someone compliments Neha on her appearance. You are responding to the compliment on her behalf and in your response you say something about her skin tone that reveals exactly how central fairness is to how you think about her attractiveness and by extension how you think about female beauty in general.
